searchLedger.hypothesis: Normal-path sentinel write precedes shard rename

searchActions.result: The call site computes quarantinePath at line 589, synchronously calls write_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel at line 590, and only then calls quarantine_vector_shard at line 591.

searchLedger.rationale: There is no normal-path move-before-sentinel ordering bug; the remaining durability issue is that the write helper catches failures and returns.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The write-then-act order is explicit and synchronous.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Temp+rename discipline matches corruption sentinel writer

searchActions.result: The repair sentinel temp path is sentinelPath plus .tmp.<pid> at line 1032, so it is in the same directory as the final sentinel. The writer writes the temp file, fsyncs it, renames it, and fsyncs the directory at lines 1033-1049. The corruption sentinel writer uses the same pattern at lines 1141-1150.

searchLedger.rationale: No cross-device rename or torn-write deviation was found relative to the existing proven corruption-sentinel pattern.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Sentinel path is confined to checkpoints and basename-derived

searchActions.result: get_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel_path derives the base directory from the vector shard directory at lines 1010-1011 and places .<basename>.repair-pending under checkpoints at lines 1012-1016. The shard filename itself is context-vectors__<profile.slug>.sqlite at line 414.

searchLedger.rationale: Using path.basename prevents path traversal through shardPath, and the profile slug in the shard filename prevents an observed cross-profile collision in this path.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No path traversal or cross-shard collision evidence in the reviewed commit.

title: Sentinel write failure is fail-open before destructive quarantine
evidence: quarantine_and_rebuild_vector_shard calls write_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel at line 590, then immediately renames the shard aside via quarantine_vector_shard at line 591 and schedules only an in-process rebuild at line 592. The writer catches all errors at lines 1050-1052 and only logs a warning, so mkdir/write/fsync/rename failures are invisible to the caller.
rationale: If the sentinel cannot be persisted, for example due to ENOSPC, EACCES, or rename failure, quarantine still moves the live shard to .quarantined-* without a durable .repair-pending marker. A restart before the scheduled rebuild completes then loses the repair intent; on the next attach, the missing shard path is treated as probe-ok at lines 507-508 unless a sentinel exists, so the phase durability claim is false in this crash window.
